_note: instance config for the catalog loop's audit phase (spec/parts/catalog-loop.md, mechanism vs config). dimensions + weights are per-store tuning; the engine knows nothing about any vertical — taxonomy.json does. `requires` names the raw signals a dimension needs: a signal the sync never landed marks the dimension not-scorable (named in the report, weight withheld — never scored zero as if measured).

classification.note: product_type resolves to a locked taxonomy leaf (curated subcategories, then the 417-leaf source map). v0 preferred the persisted commerceos.category metafield; metafields are not landed by the current sync, so this body scores from product_type alone.

specs_structured.note: at least one typed commerceos.* spec metafield (descriptive blobs excluded via spec_blob_keys).

provenance.note: every spec carries a parseable companion (source, verified, verified_on); none verified-without-source — the refuse-to-guess invariant.

identity_gtin.note: a variant barcode that is a checksum-valid GTIN-8/12/13/14 as stored — not an SKU in costume. stricter than v0, which matched digits by regex only.

merchandising.note: carries at least one tag (the signal smart collections rule on). v0 scored collection membership directly; collections are not landed by the current sync, so tags are the scorable basis.

seo.note: seo title and seo description both present.

images.note: a featured image or image list resolves — the dimension a bad push can silently empty, which is why the image scar test exists.

signals._note: raw-payload keys per signal. a key present with a null value counts as landed (requested, came back empty — a real gap); an absent key means the sync never asked — unknowable, not zero.
